walnut-cake-and-egg-jam-kitchen-robot-te.name: Walnut Cake And Egg Jam (kitchen Robot)
walnut-cake-and-egg-jam-kitchen-robot-te.original_name: Bolo de noz e doce de ovos (robot de cozinha)
instructions: Connect the oven at 180oC, grease a form with margarine and line it with vegetable paper. Place the lemon peel in the robot cup, add the nuts and sugar and start 1 minute at maximum speed.

Add eggs, margarine, yeast and flour and turn 30 seconds at speed 6 (speed 7 on Mycook). Pass with the spatula turn another 30 seconds at speed 6 (speed 7 on Mycook). Drop to the shape and put in the oven for about 40 minutes. Stick a toothpick to see if it's cooked, remove it from the oven and deform it.

Make the sweet eggs: wash and dry the glass, pour the sugar, yolks, the moreena flour and water and turn on 5 seconds at speed 4 (speed 5 on Mycook). Then program at 90oC for 4 minutes at speed 3 (speed 4 on Mycook). Water the cake and serve decorated to taste.

walnut-liqueur-argiro.name: Walnut Liqueur
instructions: We choose tender walnuts green end May by mid June.
We wash them very well.
Cut them into pieces or break them with a hammer (always with gloves because they paint the hands).
Put them in a glass jar.
Add the alcohol.
Break the nutmeg and add the carnation and cinnamon.
Seal the jar and leave it in the sun for 40 days.
After the 40-day period, we make sugar syrup with water.
When it boils, lower it from the heat and let it cool.
Add it to the alcohol with the nuts.
Leave for 15 days in the sun.
Strain in a funnel with cotton or coffee filter in sterile bottles.

watermelon-mojitos.name: Watermelon Mojitos
instructions: Place watermelon cubes into a blender; blend until smooth. Transfer to a mesh strainer set over a bowl to remove pulp. Measure 2 cups strained watermelon juice; set aside.
Place mint leaves in the bottom of a pitcher. Add ½ watermelon juice to the pitcher; mash mint leaves with a cocktail muddler or bottom of a wooden spoon.
Stir in remaining ½ watermelon juice, rum, lime juice, and simple syrup. Fill pitcher with ice; top with club soda to serve.

watermelon-feta-skewers.name: Watermelon-feta Skewers
instructions: Place 1 feta cheese cube on 1 watermelon cube; secure with a toothpick.
Repeat with remaining feta and watermelon cubes.
Sprinkle with mint.

whipped-honey-butter.name: Whipped Honey Butter
instructions: Beat butter and honey together until creamy. Serve at room temperature.

white-chocolate-mousse-with-passion-frui.name: White Chocolate Mousse With Passion Fruit
white-chocolate-mousse-with-passion-frui.original_name: Mousse de chocolate branco com maracujá
instructions: Drain the gelatin leaves in cold water for a few minutes. Break the chocolate into a bowl, add the milk and jelly drained and take to the fire in a sea bath.
Shake well until the gelatine is well dissolved and the chocolate melted. Take it out of the fire and let it cool.
On the other hand, whip cream into whipped cream and wrap it with the previous preparation. Distribute into individual bowls and lead to cold until solidifying.
When serving, open the passion fruit in half, remove the pulp with a spoon and dispose in the bowls, covering the mousse. Decorate with mint and serve.

Shake well until the gelatine is well dissolved and the chocolate melted. Take it out of the fire and let it cool.

white-chocolate-dipped-strawberries-with.name: White-chocolate-dipped Strawberries With Citrus Sugar
instructions: Line baking sheet with foil. Using fingertips, mix sugar and citrus peels in small bowl until sugar is moist. Stir chocolate in small bowl set over saucepan of barely simmering water until melted and smooth. Remove from over water.

Holding 1 strawberry by stem end, dip 2/3 of berry into chocolate; shake excess back into bowl. Turn berry dipped end up and sprinkle with citrus sugar. Place on prepared sheet. Repeat with remaining berries, chocolate, and sugar. Chill until chocolate sets, about 30 minutes.

winter-greens-laing.name: Winter Greens Laing

ingredients.item: Johnny’s seasoning salt or kosher salt

ingredients.prep: ribs and stems removed, leaves chopped
trimmed, ribs and stems removed and chopped, leaves torn
ribs and stems removed, leaves chopped
thinly sliced, plus more for serving if desired

instructions: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven or other heavy pot over medium-high. Add garlic and ginger; cook, stirring, until golden, about 1 minute. Add onion, season with seasoning salt (or kosher salt and pepper), and cook, stirring often, until onion is translucent, about 3 minutes. Add shrimp paste; cook, stirring, until slightly darkened, about 2 minutes.

Add collard greens and Swiss chard ribs and stems to pot and cook, stirring, until wilted and beginning to soften, about 3 minutes. Add kale and Swiss chard leaves; cook, stirring, until wilted, about 3 minutes. Continue to cook until all greens are tender, about 4 minutes more. Add coconut milk and 5–8 chiles, depending on how spicy your chiles are and your heat preference. Increase heat to high; bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer until laing is thickened and flavors have come together, 8–10 minutes. Stir in vinegar; season with seasoning salt (or kosher salt and pepper) if needed.
Serve laing topped with more sliced chiles if desired.
